leadText: Alomomola (Japanese: ママンボウ Mamanbou) is a Water-type Pokémon introduced in Generation V. It is not known to evolve into or from any other Pokémon.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Alomomola is a pink, fishlike Pokémon with darker pink markings on its heart-shaped body and fins. It has large dorsal and ventral fins resembling hands, each divided into four sections and tipped with a paler pink. The shape of the Pokémon resembles an ocean sunfish, as it lacks a proper tail fin. Its eyes are round with golden irises and a mark that appears to be an eyelash extending up and back toward the rest of the body. Alomomola's entire body is enveloped in a special membrane that can heal wounds. Alomomola lives in the open seas. If it finds an injured Pokémon floating at sea, it will heal the Pokémon's wounds using its membrane and carry it back to shore. Alomomola does this because any predators coming for the injured Pokémon could also come for Alomomola. Alomomola swims alongside smaller Pokémon to accompany them; thus, many water Pokémon gather around it. Fishermen take Alomomola during long voyages, saving the need to have a doctor or medicine on ships.

Generation V Unova B W : #100 Unova B2 W2 : #182 Black The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds. White Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore. Black 2 It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds. White 2 Generation VI Central Kalos #— Coastal Kalos #149 Mountain Kalos #— Hoenn #— X Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore. Y It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds. Omega Ruby Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore. Alpha Sapphire It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds. Generation VII Alola S M : #157 Alola US UM : #191 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Sun It uses its special mucus to close the wounds of injured Pokémon. The reason for this behavior remains unknown. Moon They float upon the open sea. Many water Pokémon gather in the area around Alomomola. Ultra Sun Fishermen take them along on long voyages, because if you have an Alomomola with you, there'll be no need for a doctor or medicine. Ultra Moon The reason it helps Pokémon in a weakened condition is that any Pokémon coming after them may also attack Alomomola. Its special membrane heals their wounds. Violet They float upon the open sea. Many water Pokémon gather in the area around Alomomola. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry SM033 Alomomola Rotom Pokédex Alomomola, the Caring Pokémon. A Water type. It can use Heal Pulse to help friends recover.
